questions.stem: I ______ my homework two hours ago, but I ______ it yet.
questions.explanation: 【解析】第一空后有"two hours ago"（两小时前），这是一般过去时的标志词，表示过去某个具体时间发生的动作，用finished；第二空后有"yet"（还，尚），这是现在完成时的标志词，常用于否定句和疑问句，表示动作尚未完成，用haven't checked。干扰项D虽然第二空正确，但第一空用了现在完成时，与ago矛盾；B和C的第二空都错误。【解题技巧】口诀：ago用过去，yet用完成。看到具体过去时间（yesterday, last week, ago）选过去时；看到yet, already, since, for选现在完成时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is talking to our teacher is Bosco's brother.
questions.explanation: 【解析】先行词是"The boy"（指人），且定语从句"______ is talking to our teacher"中缺少主语，因此应选关系代词who。A选项which用于指物；C选项whom虽然指人，但只能在从句中作宾语；D选项whose表示"谁的"，在从句中作定语，后需接名词。本句从句缺主语，故排除C和D。【解题技巧】定语从句选择关系词三步法：1. 看先行词指人还是指物；2. 看从句中缺什么成分（主语、宾语、定语或状语）；3. 记住：who/that指人可作主语，whom指人作宾语，which/that指物，whose表示所属关系。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: Many trees ______ by the students every spring.
questions.explanation: 【解析】主语"Many trees"（许多树）是动作"plant"（种植）的承受者，因此需要用被动语态，结构为be+过去分词，排除A和B。时间状语"every spring"（每年春天）表示经常性、习惯性的动作，应用一般现在时，故选C。D选项是一般过去时的被动语态，与every spring不符。【解题技巧】被动语态判断：1. 主语是动作的承受者（被...）；2. 结构：be动词+过去分词；3. 时态看时间标志词：every day用一般现在时被动(am/is/are done)，yesterday用一般过去时被动(was/were done)。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: —Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door? —It ______ be him. He has gone to Beijing.
questions.explanation: 【解析】根据答句"He has gone to Beijing"（他去北京了，还没回来）可知，敲门的人"不可能"是王老师。表示否定推测"不可能"用can't。A选项must表示肯定推测"一定是"；B选项may和D选项might都表示"可能"，语气较弱，但本题根据语境是完全不可能的否定推测。【解题技巧】情态动词表推测口诀：肯定推测用must（一定是），否定推测用can't（不可能是），不确定推测用may/might/could（可能是）。注意：mustn't表示"禁止"，不是"不可能"。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you are ______ with your current score, you should work harder to get a better one.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查固定搭配be satisfied with（对……满意）。句意：如果你满足于目前的分数，你应该更努力以取得更好的成绩。A选项strict常与with搭配，be strict with表示"对……严格"，但语境不符；B选项busy常与with搭配，be busy with表示"忙于……"；D选项angry常与with/at搭配，be angry with表示"对……生气"。根据后半句"work harder"（更努力）可推断前半句指"如果满足于现状"，故选C。【解题技巧】be satisfied with是中考高频短语，记住satisfied（满意的）拼写和介词with的搭配。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The weather report says it will be ______ tomorrow, so remember to take an umbrella with you.
questions.explanation: 【解析】根据后半句"take an umbrella"（带伞）可推断明天会下雨。A选项sunny（晴朗的）不需要带伞；C选项snow（雪）和D选项wind（风）都是名词，而be动词后需接形容词作表语，应为snowy和windy。B选项rainy是形容词，意为"下雨的"，符合语法和语境。【解题技巧】做语境词汇题要找准关键词（umbrella→rainy），同时注意词性（be动词后接形容词）。常见天气形容词：sunny, rainy, cloudy, snowy, windy。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We should take action to protect the ______ of wild animals.
questions.explanation: 【解析】定冠词the后应接名词，构成protect the safety of...（保护……的安全）。A选项safe是形容词；B选项safely是副词；D选项save是动词（拯救）或介词（除了）。只有C选项safety是名词形式。【解题技巧】词性转换口诀：the后接名词，形容词修饰名词，副词修饰动词/形容词，动词作谓语。safe（adj.）→ safety（n.）；save（v.）→ safety（n.）。常见名词后缀：-ty（safety, beauty），-th（health, wealth）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 【解析】考查固定搭配take part in（参加）。句意：作为学生会的一员，我很兴奋能参加这次活动。B选项take place表示"发生"；C选项take a seat表示"就座"；D选项take room表示"占用空间"，均不符合语境。【解题技巧】完形填空遇到动词+名词搭配时，要考虑固定短语。take part in是中考必考短语，part前不加冠词。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school organized a volunteer activity to help the elderly in a nearby community. As a member of the Student Union, I was excited to take (8)______ in it.

On Saturday morning, we (9)______ at the community center at 8:00. The elderly people were already waiting for us. We first divided ourselves into small groups. Each group was responsible for different tasks. Some students cleaned the rooms, some sang songs, (10)______ others chatted with the elderly.

I was in the group (11)______ helped with cleaning. While working, I noticed an old man sitting alone in the corner. I walked over and started talking to him. He told me that his children lived far away and seldom visited him. I felt sorry for him and decided to visit him (12)______.

The activity lasted for three hours. When we left, the elderly people thanked us with warm smiles. Though I felt a little tired, I was happy because I brought joy to others. This experience taught me that giving is receiving. I hope more students can join in such meaningful activities.

questions.explanation: 【解析】考查动词辨析。空格后有介词at，arrive at+小地点（到达某地）是固定搭配。B选项got需与to搭配，get to+地点；C选项reached是及物动词，直接接地点，不需要介词；D选项came需与to搭配，come to+地点。因此只有arrived符合语法要求。【解题技巧】到达某地的三种表达：arrive in（大地点）/at（小地点）；get to+地点；reach+地点（及物动词）。注意reach和arrive的用法区别。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school organized a volunteer activity to help the elderly in a nearby community. As a member of the Student Union, I was excited to take (8)______ in it.

On Saturday morning, we (9)______ at the community center at 8:00. The elderly people were already waiting for us. We first divided ourselves into small groups. Each group was responsible for different tasks. Some students cleaned the rooms, some sang songs, (10)______ others chatted with the elderly.

I was in the group (11)______ helped with cleaning. While working, I noticed an old man sitting alone in the corner. I walked over and started talking to him. He told me that his children lived far away and seldom visited him. I felt sorry for him and decided to visit him (12)______.

The activity lasted for three hours. When we left, the elderly people thanked us with warm smiles. Though I felt a little tired, I was happy because I brought joy to others. This experience taught me that giving is receiving. I hope more students can join in such meaningful activities.

questions.explanation: 【解析】考查并列连词。前文"Some students cleaned the rooms, some sang songs"（一些学生打扫房间，一些唱歌）与后文"others chatted with the elderly"（其他人陪老人聊天）是并列关系，列举不同的任务，应用and连接。A选项but表转折；B选项or表选择；D选项so表结果。【解题技巧】完形填空看到some...some...others...结构时，通常用and连接，构成"一些……一些……还有一些……"的并列列举结构。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school organized a volunteer activity to help the elderly in a nearby community. As a member of the Student Union, I was excited to take (8)______ in it.

On Saturday morning, we (9)______ at the community center at 8:00. The elderly people were already waiting for us. We first divided ourselves into small groups. Each group was responsible for different tasks. Some students cleaned the rooms, some sang songs, (10)______ others chatted with the elderly.

I was in the group (11)______ helped with cleaning. While working, I noticed an old man sitting alone in the corner. I walked over and started talking to him. He told me that his children lived far away and seldom visited him. I felt sorry for him and decided to visit him (12)______.

The activity lasted for three hours. When we left, the elderly people thanked us with warm smiles. Though I felt a little tired, I was happy because I brought joy to others. This experience taught me that giving is receiving. I hope more students can join in such meaningful activities.

questions.explanation: 【解析】考查定语从句关系词。先行词是"the group"（指物），定语从句"______ helped with cleaning"中缺少主语，因此用which或that。A选项who指人；C选项whom指人但只能作宾语；D选项what不能引导定语从句。【解题技巧】定语从句选关系词：先看先行词指人还是物（group指物），再看从句缺什么成分（缺主语）。指物作主语用which/that，指人作主语用who/that。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school organized a volunteer activity to help the elderly in a nearby community. As a member of the Student Union, I was excited to take (8)______ in it.

On Saturday morning, we (9)______ at the community center at 8:00. The elderly people were already waiting for us. We first divided ourselves into small groups. Each group was responsible for different tasks. Some students cleaned the rooms, some sang songs, (10)______ others chatted with the elderly.

I was in the group (11)______ helped with cleaning. While working, I noticed an old man sitting alone in the corner. I walked over and started talking to him. He told me that his children lived far away and seldom visited him. I felt sorry for him and decided to visit him (12)______.

The activity lasted for three hours. When we left, the elderly people thanked us with warm smiles. Though I felt a little tired, I was happy because I brought joy to others. This experience taught me that giving is receiving. I hope more students can join in such meaningful activities.

questions.explanation: 【解析】考查副词用法。此处修饰动词visit（拜访），需要用副词。A选项usual是形容词；B选项regular是形容词；C选项regularly是副词（定期地）；D选项usually是副词（通常）。根据语境"我决定定期拜访他"，regularly更符合句意，强调动作的规律性。【解题技巧】形容词变副词通常加-ly（regular→regularly）。修饰动词用副词，修饰名词用形容词。根据语境：usual（通常的），regular（定期的），此处表达定期看望老人，选regularly。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school organized a volunteer activity to help the elderly in a nearby community. As a member of the Student Union, I was excited to take (8)______ in it.

On Saturday morning, we (9)______ at the community center at 8:00. The elderly people were already waiting for us. We first divided ourselves into small groups. Each group was responsible for different tasks. Some students cleaned the rooms, some sang songs, (10)______ others chatted with the elderly.

I was in the group (11)______ helped with cleaning. While working, I noticed an old man sitting alone in the corner. I walked over and started talking to him. He told me that his children lived far away and seldom visited him. I felt sorry for him and decided to visit him (12)______.

The activity lasted for three hours. When we left, the elderly people thanked us with warm smiles. Though I felt a little tired, I was happy because I brought joy to others. This experience taught me that giving is receiving. I hope more students can join in such meaningful activities.

questions.stem: Where did scientists first discover the PETase enzyme?
options: B. In a waste recycling center.
questions.explanation: 【解析】细节理解题。根据文章第二段第一句"In 2016, researchers in Japan discovered an enzyme called PETase in a waste recycling center."可知，科学家是在一个废品回收中心（waste recycling center）首次发现这种酶的。A选项在海洋中是塑料垃圾最终去向；C选项实验室是后续改良的地方；D选项工厂是公司可能应用的场所。【解题技巧】细节题要找准关键词（PETase, discovered），回原文定位到具体句子，注意同义转换（first discover对应discovered...in 2016）。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Plastic waste has become a serious environmental problem. Every year, millions of tons of plastic end up in oceans and landfills. However, a group of scientists has found a new way to deal with this problem—using special enzymes (酶) to break down plastic quickly.

In 2016, researchers in Japan discovered an enzyme called PETase in a waste recycling center. This enzyme can break down PET plastic, which is commonly used in water bottles. Recently, scientists have improved this enzyme to work much faster. The new version can break down plastic in just a few days, while in nature, plastic takes hundreds of years to decompose.

This breakthrough has excited environmentalists around the world. "If we can produce this enzyme on a large scale, it could revolutionize how we recycle plastic," said Dr. Sarah Chen, a leading researcher. However, she also pointed out that this technology is still in the early stages. More tests are needed before it can be used widely.

Despite these challenges, many people are hopeful. Some companies have already shown interest in using this technology. They believe it could help reduce the "white pollution" that harms our planet. As one environmentalist said, "Nature has given us a solution; we just need to use it wisely."

questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Sarah Chen's words?
options: A. The enzyme is ready for mass production now.
B. The technology needs more development.
C. Plastic pollution is no longer a problem.
D. Scientists should stop studying enzymes.
questions.explanation: 【解析】推理判断题。根据第三段Dr. Sarah Chen的话"this technology is still in the early stages. More tests are needed before it can be used widely"（这项技术仍处于早期阶段，在广泛应用前还需要更多测试）可推断，这项技术还需要进一步发展和完善。A选项与原文"still in the early stages"矛盾；C选项与第一段"serious environmental problem"矛盾；D选项与科学家的研究行为矛盾。【解题技巧】推理题不能选原文直接陈述的，要选言下之意。关键词：early stages（早期阶段）→ needs more development（需要更多发展）；more tests needed（需要更多测试）→ not ready now（尚未准备好）。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Plastic waste has become a serious environmental problem. Every year, millions of tons of plastic end up in oceans and landfills. However, a group of scientists has found a new way to deal with this problem—using special enzymes (酶) to break down plastic quickly.

In 2016, researchers in Japan discovered an enzyme called PETase in a waste recycling center. This enzyme can break down PET plastic, which is commonly used in water bottles. Recently, scientists have improved this enzyme to work much faster. The new version can break down plastic in just a few days, while in nature, plastic takes hundreds of years to decompose.

This breakthrough has excited environmentalists around the world. "If we can produce this enzyme on a large scale, it could revolutionize how we recycle plastic," said Dr. Sarah Chen, a leading researcher. However, she also pointed out that this technology is still in the early stages. More tests are needed before it can be used widely.

Despite these challenges, many people are hopeful. Some companies have already shown interest in using this technology. They believe it could help reduce the "white pollution" that harms our planet. As one environmentalist said, "Nature has given us a solution; we just need to use it wisely."

questions.stem: What does the underlined word "decompose" in Paragraph 2 probably mean?
questions.explanation: 【解析】词义猜测题。根据第二段对比结构"The new version can break down plastic in just a few days, while in nature, plastic takes hundreds of years to decompose"可知，while表示对比，decompose与前面的break down（分解）意思相近。酶能在几天内break down塑料，而自然状态下需要数百年才能decompose，因此decompose意为"分解"。B选项produce（生产）、C选项collect（收集）、D选项transport（运输）均不符合语境。【解题技巧】词义猜测题找逻辑关系：1. 看前后是否有对比（while, but）或并列（and, or）关系；2. 找同义词或反义词线索；3. 根据上下文语境（塑料被酶分解）推断。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Plastic waste has become a serious environmental problem. Every year, millions of tons of plastic end up in oceans and landfills. However, a group of scientists has found a new way to deal with this problem—using special enzymes (酶) to break down plastic quickly.

In 2016, researchers in Japan discovered an enzyme called PETase in a waste recycling center. This enzyme can break down PET plastic, which is commonly used in water bottles. Recently, scientists have improved this enzyme to work much faster. The new version can break down plastic in just a few days, while in nature, plastic takes hundreds of years to decompose.

This breakthrough has excited environmentalists around the world. "If we can produce this enzyme on a large scale, it could revolutionize how we recycle plastic," said Dr. Sarah Chen, a leading researcher. However, she also pointed out that this technology is still in the early stages. More tests are needed before it can be used widely.

Despite these challenges, many people are hopeful. Some companies have already shown interest in using this technology. They believe it could help reduce the "white pollution" that harms our planet. As one environmentalist said, "Nature has given us a solution; we just need to use it wisely."

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: C. A New Way to Recycle Plastic
D. The Dangers of White Pollution
questions.explanation: 【解析】主旨大意题。文章第一段提出问题（塑料污染），第二段介绍新方法（酶分解塑料），第三段讲技术现状，第四段讲应用前景。全文核心是介绍一种利用酶分解塑料的新型回收方式。A选项只涉及塑料历史，偏离主题；B选项只涉及日本科学家的发现，过于片面；D选项只讲白色污染的危害，是背景介绍而非主旨。C选项"一种回收塑料的新方法"概括全文。【解题技巧】主旨题/标题题要抓住"高频词"和"首尾段"。本文高频词：enzyme, plastic, recycle, break down。排除法：太具体的（B）、太宽泛的（D）、偏离主题的（A）都不选，选能概括主要内容的（C）。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Plastic waste has become a serious environmental problem. Every year, millions of tons of plastic end up in oceans and landfills. However, a group of scientists has found a new way to deal with this problem—using special enzymes (酶) to break down plastic quickly.

In 2016, researchers in Japan discovered an enzyme called PETase in a waste recycling center. This enzyme can break down PET plastic, which is commonly used in water bottles. Recently, scientists have improved this enzyme to work much faster. The new version can break down plastic in just a few days, while in nature, plastic takes hundreds of years to decompose.

This breakthrough has excited environmentalists around the world. "If we can produce this enzyme on a large scale, it could revolutionize how we recycle plastic," said Dr. Sarah Chen, a leading researcher. However, she also pointed out that this technology is still in the early stages. More tests are needed before it can be used widely.

Despite these challenges, many people are hopeful. Some companies have already shown interest in using this technology. They believe it could help reduce the "white pollution" that harms our planet. As one environmentalist said, "Nature has given us a solution; we just need to use it wisely."

options: A. However, spending all day studying is not healthy.
B. For example, you can do some stretching exercises or go for a walk.
C. As a result, you will feel more energetic and focused.
D. In addition, exercise helps reduce stress and improve sleep quality.
E. On the other hand, too much exercise may affect your study time.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查转折关系。前文提到"Many students struggle to balance their study time and physical exercise"（许多学生难以平衡学习和运动），后文应该转折指出只学习不运动的问题。A选项"然而，整天学习并不健康"承上启下，引出运动的必要性。B选项是举例，C选项是结果，D选项是递进，E选项是另一方面，均不适合放在开头段落的转折位置。【解题技巧】选句子填空看逻辑：However表转折（然而），For example表举例，As a result表结果，In addition表递进，On the other hand表另一方面。根据上下文逻辑关系选择。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students struggle to balance their study time and physical exercise. They often wonder how to find time for sports when they have so much homework to do. (17)______

In fact, moderate exercise can actually improve your study efficiency. When you exercise, your brain gets more oxygen and blood flow. (18)______ 

(19)______ Therefore, it is important to plan your time wisely. You can schedule short exercise breaks between study sessions. (20)______ 

Remember, a healthy body supports a healthy mind. By finding the right balance, you can achieve better results in both your studies and physical health.

options: A. However, spending all day studying is not healthy.
B. For example, you can do some stretching exercises or go for a walk.
C. As a result, you will feel more energetic and focused.
D. In addition, exercise helps reduce stress and improve sleep quality.
E. On the other hand, too much exercise may affect your study time.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查因果关系。前文"When you exercise, your brain gets more oxygen and blood flow"（当你运动时，大脑获得更多氧气和血液流动）是原因，后文应该是结果。C选项"因此，你会感到更有活力、更专注"是前文带来的结果，As a result（因此）表示因果关系。【解题技巧】前因后果选As a result/Therefore/So。前文讲运动对大脑的生理好处（oxygen, blood flow），后文讲主观感受（energetic, focused），构成因果关系。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students struggle to balance their study time and physical exercise. They often wonder how to find time for sports when they have so much homework to do. (17)______

In fact, moderate exercise can actually improve your study efficiency. When you exercise, your brain gets more oxygen and blood flow. (18)______ 

(19)______ Therefore, it is important to plan your time wisely. You can schedule short exercise breaks between study sessions. (20)______ 

Remember, a healthy body supports a healthy mind. By finding the right balance, you can achieve better results in both your studies and physical health.

options: A. However, spending all day studying is not healthy.
B. For example, you can do some stretching exercises or go for a walk.
C. As a result, you will feel more energetic and focused.
D. In addition, exercise helps reduce stress and improve sleep quality.
E. On the other hand, too much exercise may affect your study time.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查递进关系。前文讲运动能提高学习效率（improve your study efficiency），本段继续讲运动的其他好处（reduce stress and improve sleep quality），是递进关系。D选项"此外，运动有助于减轻压力和改善睡眠质量"符合语境，In addition（此外，而且）表示递进。E选项虽然也是补充内容，但讲的是运动的负面影响，与后文"Therefore, it is important to plan your time wisely"（因此，明智地规划时间很重要）衔接不如D选项自然。【解题技巧】In addition/Furthermore/Moreover表递进，补充说明同类事物的另一个优点。前文讲学习效率，此处讲压力睡眠，都是运动的益处，属递进关系。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students struggle to balance their study time and physical exercise. They often wonder how to find time for sports when they have so much homework to do. (17)______

In fact, moderate exercise can actually improve your study efficiency. When you exercise, your brain gets more oxygen and blood flow. (18)______ 

(19)______ Therefore, it is important to plan your time wisely. You can schedule short exercise breaks between study sessions. (20)______ 

Remember, a healthy body supports a healthy mind. By finding the right balance, you can achieve better results in both your studies and physical health.

options: A. However, spending all day studying is not healthy.
B. For example, you can do some stretching exercises or go for a walk.
C. As a result, you will feel more energetic and focused.
D. In addition, exercise helps reduce stress and improve sleep quality.
E. On the other hand, too much exercise may affect your study time.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查举例关系。前文"You can schedule short exercise breaks between study sessions"（你可以在学习间隙安排短暂的运动休息）提出了建议，后文应该举例说明具体怎么做。B选项"例如，你可以做一些拉伸运动或散步"是对前文的举例说明，For example（例如）是标志词。【解题技巧】看到For example/For instance/such as要想到前文是总述，后文是具体例子。前文讲schedule short exercise breaks，后文讲stretching exercises or go for a walk，是具体例子。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students struggle to balance their study time and physical exercise. They often wonder how to find time for sports when they have so much homework to do. (17)______

In fact, moderate exercise can actually improve your study efficiency. When you exercise, your brain gets more oxygen and blood flow. (18)______ 

(19)______ Therefore, it is important to plan your time wisely. You can schedule short exercise breaks between study sessions. (20)______ 

Remember, a healthy body supports a healthy mind. By finding the right balance, you can achieve better results in both your studies and physical health.
